title: profile and company are async polled scrapes (tens of seconds to minutes) — only search is instant
summary: Timing differs sharply by mode and the agent must set the right expectation. profile TRIGGERS a Bright Data dataset scrape then polls the snapshot ~1x/sec with NO wall-clock cap (the dispatcher decides when to give up) — commonly 20s-2min. company starts an Apify actor run then polls ~1x/2sec, also uncapped — minutes, scaling with max_employees and profile_mode. search is the only one-shot mode: a single Google-SERP call (60s worker timeout) that returns in seconds. So: poll no faster than every 3-5s (or use SSE), tell the user 'a few moments / minutes' for profile+company, and never tight-loop — polling faster does not make the upstream scrape finish sooner.
references: windmill-scripts/spiderpeople_profile.py:123-218 — triggers snapshot, polls every POLL_INTERVAL=1s, while True (no cap)
windmill-scripts/spiderpeople_company.py:69,157-224 — Apify run, POLL_INTERVAL=2s, while True (no cap)
